title: The river rising — Wend lights the lantern alone
The house fills — twelve strangers stranded
The warden's empty cell — Mabon is not here
Berdine carries the strongbox to his cell
The hooded traveller takes the far cell, gives no name
Supper in the common room — the tinker speaks of the warden
The clerk shows his sealed papers to no one, but Wend sees the seal
First flood night — a cry, a crash, then the river
Morning — the strongbox broken open, the coin gone
Berdine names the hooded traveller as the thief
The midwife found a wet cloak — and says nothing yet
The soldier and the drover quarrel over the watch order
Second night — the tinker tells Wend the warden owed a debt
The deed-bond — Wend finds the chain-ferry log torn out
The body in the millrace — Mabon is found
Wend confronts the hooded traveller; the truth of the box surfaces
Third dawn — the water begins to fall, and Wend must choose
NAME — Wend lays the whole matter before the clerk
NAME — the clerk's sealed papers prove the deed-bond a forgery
NAME — Berdine's staged theft unravels under the clerk's questions
NAME terminal — Cray and Berdine bound for the assize; Wend keeps the ford
HOLD — Wend says nothing, lets the house cross
HOLD — the young couple cross first, the soldier last
HOLD — Wend burns the deed-bond he took from the body
HOLD terminal — the ford stands clear; Cray gone, the debt with him; Wend alone with what he knows
ACT — Wend raises the chain-ferry and shuts the ford against Cray
ACT — the soldier and the tinker stand with Wend; Cray is cornered
ACT — the deed-bond burned at the lantern; Cray confesses to Mabon
ACT terminal — Cray drowns at the ford he came to take; the house crosses, none the wiser
